metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_353
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Gronborg, M et al compared the protein composition of synaptic vesicles enriched either in VGLUT-1 or VGAT by mass spectrometry and validated the data by means of microscopy and immunoblotting. Among the differentially distributed proteins between the two SV subpopulations, MAL2 protein was identified as preferentially enriched in the VGLUT1-containing SV fracton (Fig. 2B and Table 1 - quantitative proteomic comparison of proteins from immunoisolated SVs).
MAL2, predicted to contain four transmembrane domains, was mainly enriched in the VGLUT-1-specific SVs as shown by immunoblotting with newly raised antibody (Fig. 3, Fig 6 C-E): Pronase treatment of synaptic vesicles (Stenius et al.1995) and Triton X-114 extraction (Bordier 1981) helped identifying MAL2 as integral protein in the SVs with its N-terminus exposed in the cytoplasm and not into the vesicular lumen. MAL2 was strongly enriched in subcellular fractionation of whole rat brain similar to synaptophysin, Rab3a, synaptotagmin-1 and VGLUT-2 in the CPG-SV fraction (Fig. 6C). This finding was confirmed by EM immunogold double labeling of MAL2 and VGLUT-1 (Fig. 6D). MAL2 showed preferential expression in brain and liver as surveyed by immunoblotting of rat tissue samples (Fig. 6E).
Finally, double immunofluorescent labeling of MAL2 with either VGLUT-1 or VGAT in primary cultures of hippocampal neurons (Fig. 7) as well as in mouse and rat hippocampal and cerebellar sections (Fig. 8 A-D) confirmed selective but not exclusive presence of MAL2 in glutamatergic neurons. Double immunogold labeling of MAL2 and VGLUT-1 in mossy fiber terminals (rat CA3 area) (Fig. 8E, Fig S4 C-D) indicated vesicular colocalization of both proteins on SVs.. Experimental description: Materials&Methods
Immunoisolation.
Affinity purified VGLUT-1 or VGAT rabbit antibodies were conjugated to Eupergit C1Z methacrylate microbeads (Röhm Pharmaceuticals) and used for immunoisolation as described previously (Burger et al., 1989; Takamori et al., 2000a,b).

Tissue preparation (immunocytochemistry).
For all procedures ethical approval was obtained in accordance with the National Act on the Use of Experimental Animals (Germany). Male adult Wistar rats (300-350 g) were anesthetized by a mixture of medetomidine (500 μg/kg, Pfizer, Orion Pharma) and ketamine (75 mg/kg, WDT) and perfused following a protocol given by Takumi et al. (1999).
